warning: POC scaffolding. No legal verification. Do not use for execution. See docs/POC-LIMITATIONS.md.

routingAxes.askPrompt: Which Finnish Wolt legal entity is the employer?
routingAxes.description: Selects the registered legal-entity boilerplate (name, business ID, registered address).
Determines term clause shape and trial-period max.

routingAxes.askPrompt: What tier does this role belong to?
routingAxes.description: Tier controls whether IP / Confidentiality / Non-compete / Non-solicit / Liquidated-damages clause group applies. Operational roles (e.g. Grocery Associate) historically omit these; supervisor and specialist roles include them.
Controls working-hours clause shape and weekend/night premium clauses.

routingAxes.askPrompt: Hourly pay-scale or fixed monthly salary?
routingAxes.description: Operational roles historically use hourly pay-grade (e.g. B2 scale); supervisor/specialist use fixed monthly EUR.

slots.intentNote: Identifies the employee party to the agreement.
Statutorily required identifier for the employee party. Sensitive — never auto-fetched.
Some FIN templates list DOB alongside address as identifying info. Convention varies by template.
Identifying info for the employee party; treated as sensitive PII.
Commencement date of the employment relationship.

slots.askPrompt: When does the fixed-term contract end?
slots.intentNote: Required only for fixed-term contracts.

slots.askPrompt: Justified grounds for the fixed-term contract?
slots.intentNote: Fixed-term contracts in FIN require a documented grounds. Common observed examples include project-based work, seasonal peak, substitution, internship.

slots.askPrompt: If a permanent internal hire, when did the prior employment relationship start?
slots.intentNote: Used in the 'permanent internal hire' conditional path; affects annual-leave accrual continuity.
Trial period max varies by term type — fixed-term contracts cannot exceed half the term.

slots.askPrompt: Where will they primarily work? (city, metropolitan area, or specific office)
slots.intentNote: Free-form work location. Some FIN templates enumerate cities (Helsinki/Tampere/Oulu/Turku); others use a metropolitan area; supervisor templates often specify Helsinki metropolitan area. The downstream renderer classifies the value as needed.
Finnish standard full-time is 37.5h/week.

slots.askPrompt: Weekend premium (% of base salary per shift)?
slots.intentNote: Observed in shiftwork templates as 8% per weekend shift, subject to statutory Sunday-work minimums.
The Employee's job title at the start of employment.

slots.askPrompt: Specific duties (for operational roles, a short bullet list)?
slots.intentNote: Operational templates enumerate concrete duties (taking orders, packing, inventory, etc.); supervisor/specialist templates use generic 'all duties of the position'.
Operational roles use a CBA pay-grade reference.
Embedded in operational templates alongside the pay-grade reference.
Supervisor/specialist templates state a fixed monthly EUR figure.

slots.askPrompt: Additional paid days off beyond statutory minimum?
slots.intentNote: Some templates grant additional leave days above the Annual Holidays Act baseline.
Observed: 'Commercial sector's collective agreement (warehouse workers)' for Grocery Associate; 'Warehouse and Transportation Supervisors' for Store Manager. Should be derived from role + entity.
Only applies when senior_protections clause group is active.
Only applies when senior_protections clause group is active.

baselineSections.intentNote: Identifies employer and employee as parties.
Defines duration and validity of the employment relationship.
Hours of work and overtime/weekend terms.

clauseGroups.description: Right-to-work warranty and consequences if entitlement lapses.
clauseIntents: Employee warrants entitlement to work in Finland
Employee shall provide evidence on Company request
Postponement of start if right-to-work missing
Termination grounds if right-to-work is lost during employment

clauseGroups.description: Clause bundle that historically applies to non-operational roles only.
clauseIntents: Intellectual Property assignment to Company
Confidentiality and trade-secret protection
Access-to-email consent for Company
Non-competition during employment (refers Employment Contracts Act Chapter 3 Section 3)
Non-solicitation of personnel and clients for 6 months post-termination
Liquidated damages of 6 months' salary for breach of the above
Governing law (Finland) and jurisdiction (District Court of Helsinki)

clauseGroups.description: Premium-pay and rest-time provisions for shiftwork.
clauseIntents: Shifts arranged across the week, subject to operating hours
Sunday and public-holiday work consent
Weekend premium (default 8% per shift, subject to statutory minimums)
Overtime agreed in advance in writing
Statutory rest-time and break entitlement

clauseGroups.description: Additional terms only present in fixed-term contracts.
clauseIntents: Fixed-term end date and documented grounds
Trial-period max may not exceed half the term

clauseGroups.description: Conversion of a prior fixed-term relationship into a permanent one.
clauseIntents: Acknowledges supersession of prior agreement
Acknowledges prior employment commencement date for annual-leave continuity
